political.s: Arby's is privately held by Inspire Brands (owned by Roark Capital Group) and does not file FEC disclosures as a company. As a private entity, direct corporate PAC contributions are not tracked, though historical data from when parent company Wendy's/Arby's Group was public showed minimal political engagement. Private company status means limited transparency on political spending.

charity.s: Arby's Foundation has donated over $100 million to youth-related causes over 30+ years, with focus on hunger relief and career readiness. In 2015, raised $3.7 million for No Kid Hungry campaign. Company supports community initiatives through PurposeFULL CSR platform emphasizing youth empowerment and local philanthropy.
charity.focus: Youth empowerment, hunger relief, career development

environment.s: Achieved 15.2% energy reduction and 8.6% water reduction in company-owned restaurants since 2011, generating $20.4 million in cost savings. Joined Better Buildings Challenge in 2015. However, identified as doing 'poor' job reducing plastic waste by As You Sow and NRDC, and lacks meaningful policies on antibiotic use in beef supply.

labor.s: Glassdoor ratings show compensation/benefits in bottom 10-15% of similar companies (2.7-2.8/5 stars). Starting wages reported at $10.50-$13.30/hour with minimal raises. Company highlights 'Start Here, Go Anywhere' people development program, but employee reviews indicate limited wage growth and concerns about management practices.

dei.s: Glassdoor DEI rating of 3.5/5 stars (sector average). Company emphasizes diversity in leadership across Inspire Brands portfolio but faces industry-wide pressure on DEI initiatives. No specific anti-DEI stance identified, maintaining neutral middle-ground approach.

animals.s: Sources indicate limited animal welfare commitments. Received 'F' grade in 2024 report for failing to restrict antibiotics in beef supply. Criticized by Mercy for Animals for inadequate chicken welfare standards compared to competitors. Company sources eggs from cage farms despite industry trend toward cage-free. Member of US Roundtable for Sustainable Beef but lacks comprehensive poultry welfare policy.

guns.s: No information found regarding firearms sales or manufacturing. As a fast-food restaurant chain, guns are not relevant to business operations.

privacy.s: Arby's privacy policy allows collection of personal information including contact, payment, demographic, and location data. Company shares data with third parties for targeted advertising and marketing purposes. Offers California consumer rights (access, delete, correct) and opt-out mechanisms for targeted ads, but practice of sharing for behavioral advertising indicates mixed privacy protections.

execPay.s: Limited disclosure available due to private company status. Reported average executive compensation around $233,000 annually, while average employee wage is $47,266 (as of March 2026). Estimated CEO-to-worker pay ratio approximately 5:1 based on available data, though comprehensive SEC proxy data unavailable.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
